title: The Power of Abortion Policy: Re-examining the Effects of Young Women's Access to Reproductive Control
exclusion_reason: Paper-auditor FAIL (gap 60%). Paper's Table 2 Panel A Col 1 baseline is multivariate with 6 reproductive policy variables + state linear trends + race controls; our pipeline can only fit a univariate TWFE on epillconsent18 alone. Same outcome, same data, but estimand is fundamentally different. Excluded 2026-04-19 from comparable subsample (N=53). Methodology reanalysis still recorded in skeptic_report.md for reference.
notes: Myers (2017) JPE. TWFE uses CONTINUOUS epillconsent18 (fraction of years 14-17 with pill consent, values 0/0.25/0.50/0.75/1.00) matching original specification. CS/SA/Gardner use DISCRETIZED binary pillconsent18 for group identification. Original multivariate spec includes 6 policy exposures + state trends — our univariate TWFE gives -0.0053 vs original -0.0084 (both insig). Pre-aggregated from 272K individual CPS obs to 49 states x 24 birth-cohorts (1935-1958) panel (weighted mean). 2 non-absorbing states (28,30) excluded. 9 treatment cohorts (1942-1956), 3 never-treated (29,31,56). Outcome: probability of first birth before age 19. EVENT STUDY REVIEWED: All 4 estimators (TWFE, CS-NT, SA, Gardner) show null results at every horizon — point estimates bounce around ±0.01, all CIs include zero. No pre-trends, no post-treatment effect. Classic null. The +350% TWFE-vs-CS ratio is meaningless (both ≈0).
